mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 24-month rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the threshold signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term interest rates combined with a stable federal funds rate indicates improving real economic momentum without financial tightening, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect rapid capacity utilization and earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 70% of recovery-phase months, or the mechanism reverses direction during standard expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals expanding economic activity and wage growth, which supports corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 24-month rolling window, or payroll growth shows no statistical correlation with 20-day forward returns in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5% indicates a sharp contraction in construction demand and related supply chains, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months following a greater than 5% housing starts decline, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for interest rates.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a rising unemployment rate signals stagflationary pressure, forcing monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces positive forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term lacks predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion regimes, quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms resilient aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The signal predicts negative returns in more than 65% of late-cycle quarters, or the mechanism loses significance when GDP growth falls below 1.5%.
